sentences: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Programming is the art of telling a computer what to do.
A function takes an input and produces an output.
Every great developer was once a beginner who refused to give up.
The best way to learn to code is to write a lot of code.
Clean code is not written by following a set of rules.
First make it work, then make it right, then make it fast.
A bug is never just a mistake, it represents something bigger.
The most disastrous thing you can ever learn is your first programming language.
Any fool can write code that a computer can understand.
Good code is its own best documentation.
Simplicity is the soul of efficiency in software development.
Before software can be reusable it first has to be usable.
The function of good software is to make the complex appear simple.
It is not enough for code to work, it must communicate clearly.
When in doubt, leave it out and keep your codebase clean.
Software is a great combination of artistry and engineering.
The best programmers are not the ones who know the most syntax.
Typing faster does not make you a better programmer, thinking clearly does.
Every line of code you write is a decision you have to defend.
A keyboard is the painter's brush for the modern developer.
Debugging is twice as hard as writing the code in the first place.
If you cannot explain it simply, you do not understand it well enough.
The computer was born to solve problems that did not exist before.
Code is like humor, when you have to explain it it is bad.
Walking on water and developing software from a specification are easy if both are frozen.
The most important property of a program is whether it accomplishes the intention of its user.
Always code as if the person who ends up maintaining your code is a violent psychopath.
Software undergoes beta testing shortly before it is released and then it goes gold.
There are only two kinds of programming languages, the ones people complain about and the ones nobody uses.
Measuring programming progress by lines of code is like measuring aircraft building progress by weight.
The amateur software engineer is always in search of magic, some special technique or tool that will make the process of software design easy.
Most of you are familiar with the virtues of a programmer, there are three of course: laziness, impatience, and hubris.
The problem with using a language that is too powerful is that it makes your problem look simple.
One of the best programming skills you can have is knowing when to walk away and come back later.
Computers are good at following instructions but not at reading your mind.
The best error message is the one that never shows up.
Make it work, make it right, make it fast, in that order always.
Do not repeat yourself, every piece of knowledge should have a single representation.
Software is never finished, only abandoned at a point where it is good enough.
An API is a contract between two pieces of software about how they will talk to each other.
Version control is not just a backup, it is a history of every decision you made.
A good commit message is a letter to your future self explaining why you made a change.
The terminal is your best friend once you stop being afraid of it.
Learning to read error messages carefully is one of the most valuable skills in programming.
A server is just someone else's computer that is always turned on and connected to the internet.
JSON stands for JavaScript Object Notation and is used everywhere data is exchanged on the web.
An HTTP request is a message from a client asking a server to do something or give something back.
A database is just a very organised and very fast way of storing and retrieving information.
The internet is a network of computers all agreeing to speak the same language to each other.
